text.title: core-vui-design-principles Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for core-vui-design-principles as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM over-acknowledgment: "Sure! I can absolutely do that for you! Let me check that right away!"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Filler acknowledgment inflates latency — every word adds approximately 50ms TTS overhead — and feels sycophantic. One-word acknowledgment ("Got it.") or no acknowledgment before the answer is preferred.

failure-mode.description: Context window exhaustion causes the agent to ask the user to repeat information already provided earlier in the session.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Context limits (approximately 30k tokens for Realtime API) are a design constraint; the session structure must proactively summarize and compress prior context before limits are hit.

failure-mode.description: LLM over-acknowledges every user input with affirmations like "Great!", "Of course!", "Sure thing!", "Absolutely!", "I'd be happy to help!".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each filler word adds approximately 50 milliseconds of TTS overhead. When these accumulate across a session, latency quickly exceeds the 1.5 second threshold. Additionally, excessive affirmations signal non-human origin and feel performative rather than natural. The solution is explicit instruction in the system prompt to omit acknowledgment and go directly to answering the question.

failure-mode.description: Simplicity principle applied indiscriminately to chat interfaces or visual UI copy, producing text that is too terse for reading contexts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: VUI simplicity rules are calibrated specifically for audio processing with auditory working memory constraints. Those constraints do not apply to text. A 10-word sentence that is ideal for voice can be insufficient for a UI button label or form instruction. Apply the principles only to voice interactions.

failure-mode.description: Developers apply these VUI principles to mandatory regulatory disclosure scripts (banking disclosures, healthcare consent forms, tax warnings).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Regulatory disclosures must be verbatim per legal requirements. The principles of "natural conversation" and "simplicity" cannot override legal text requirements. When regulatory text is required, disable the VUI principles for that segment and read the legal text as-is, even if it violates the rules.
